mapping.a_element: provisional macro-frame set by the initial prompt (problem, representations, assumptions, criteria)
mapping.b_element: persistent long-horizon memory of the scene

mapping.a_element: each subsequent learner turn maintaining/specifying/challenging/repairing/transforming the frame
prompt framing trajectory (the ordered sequence of prompt-response-uptake turns)
mapping.b_element: the stream of incoming per-frame video observations over time

brief_justification: The only overlap is a loose metaphor of maintaining and iteratively updating a persistent state across a sequence (frames vs. prompt turns), not shared transferable machinery: paper A is a qualitative epistemological/educational framework about human interpretation and disciplinary framing of LLM prompts, whereas paper B is a concrete deep-learning system defined by gradient-based Test-Time-Training memory updates, cross-view attention, an auxiliary memory loss, and caching to prevent catastrophic drift. There is no common governing equation, threshold, or estimation/optimization process linking them, so no role-for-role mechanism mapping holds.
